w:r.w:t: This is to certify that the “
End-to-End E-Commerce Web Application with Payment Gateway (Elegance Perfumes)
the partial fulfillment of the Term-work of Second year in Information Technology
University at Pune Vidyarthi Griha’s College of Engineering, Technology & Management, Pune under Savitribai Phule, Pune University, Pune. This work is done during Semester I, Academic Year 2025-26.

The rapid growth of internet technology and digital platforms has significantly transformed the way businesses operate. E-commerce applications have become an essential component of modern business systems, enabling users to purchase products and services online without geographical limitations. These systems provide convenience, efficiency, and accessibility to both customers and businesses.

The project titled “Elegance Perfumes” is a web-based e-commerce application developed to simulate a real-world online shopping environment. The system enables users to browse products, filter them based on preferences, add items to a shopping cart, and complete the purchase process using a secure checkout mechanism.

Unlike traditional e-commerce systems that rely heavily on backend servers such as PHP or Node.js, this project adopts a modern frontend-first architecture. It leverages Supabase, a Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) platform, to handle critical backend functionalities such as authentication, database management, and file storage. This approach simplifies development while maintaining performance and scalability.

The application also integrates a QR-based UPI payment system, allowing users to complete transactions using digital payment apps such as Google Pay. Instead of automatic payment verification, a manual verification system is implemented where users upload payment proof, and an administrator reviews and approves or rejects the transaction.

Additionally, the system includes an admin dashboard, enabling administrators to manage orders, verify payments, and maintain system integrity. Security is ensured using Row Level Security (RLS) policies, which restrict data access based on user roles.

This project serves as a comprehensive demonstration of modern web development practices, combining frontend technologies, backend services, authentication systems, and payment workflows into a unified application.

The primary aim of this project is to design and develop a fully functional end-to-end e-commerce web application that simulates real-world online shopping systems. The project focuses on providing a smooth and interactive user experience while integrating modern web technologies and backend services.

This application enables users to browse products, add items to a shopping cart, and complete purchases using a secure checkout process. It also demonstrates how a complete e-commerce workflow can be implemented without using a traditional backend server, by utilizing Supabase as a Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) platform.

Another important aim of this project is to implement a digital payment mechanism using a QR-based UPI system. This approach provides practical exposure to payment workflows and transaction handling while keeping the system simple and suitable for academic purposes.
The project also includes an administrative module that allows monitoring and management of orders, ensuring that the system represents a complete and realistic e-commerce environment.

The project aims to create an interactive and responsive user interface using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The interface is designed to ensure easy navigation, clear product display, and a smooth user experience across different devices.
2. To Implement Product Browsing and Filtering

The system provides functionality for users to explore products efficiently. Users can search for items and filter them based on criteria such as price, which enhances usability and improves the overall shopping experience.
3. To Design a Shopping Cart System

A dynamic shopping cart is implemented to allow users to add, remove, and update items. The cart data is stored using localStorage, ensuring persistence even after page refresh, without requiring backend storage.

The application uses Supabase Authentication to provide secure login and registration features. It supports email/password login as well as Google OAuth, ensuring that only authorized users can access sensitive functionalities.
5. To Implement Checkout and Payment System

The project includes a checkout system where users can review their order and proceed to payment. A QR-based UPI payment system is used, allowing users to complete transactions using external payment applications.
6. To Use Supabase for Backend Services
Supabase is used to manage backend operations such as:
This eliminates the need for traditional backend development while maintaining efficiency and scalability.
Security is implemented using Row Level Security (RLS) policies, ensuring that users can access only their own data while administrators have broader access for management purposes.
An admin interface is created to manage orders and verify payments. The admin can approve or reject transactions and maintain control over the system.
The development of this project helps achieve the following course outcomes:
Knowledge of frontend and backend integration
Understanding of database systems and cloud services
Ability to design and develop real-world web applications
Implementation of authentication and session handling
Working with APIs and cloud-based backend services
Handling real-world constraints such as payment verification
The frontend of the application is responsible for user interaction and interface design.
HTML is used to structure the web pages of the application. It defines elements such as:
CSS is used for styling and enhancing the visual appearance of the application:
User interface components such as buttons and cards
JavaScript is used to add dynamic behavior to the application:
Although traditional backend technologies like PHP or Node.js are commonly used, this project uses a modern alternative.
Supabase provides all backend functionalities without requiring a custom server.
Row Level Security (RLS) for data protection
The application uses a structured relational database provided by Supabase.
Stores order details, payment info, and status
The system uses a UPI QR-based payment approach:
User scans QR using payment app
This method is simple and suitable for academic and prototype systems.

This project involves the design and development of a complete e-commerce web application named “Elegance Perfumes”, which simulates a real-world online shopping system. The work carried out in this project is divided into multiple modules, each responsible for a specific functionality of the system. The application follows a frontend-first architecture with backend services handled using Supabase.
The major components and their implementation details are described below:
The first phase of the project involved identifying system requirements and defining the scope of the application.
A clear workflow was designed to ensure smooth interaction between different modules of the system.
After requirement analysis, the system architecture and design were planned.
The system was designed to operate without a traditional backend server, with all backend functionalities handled through Supabase APIs.
The frontend of the application was developed using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Displays product catalog and cart functionality
Displays order summary and payment interface
The product catalog is implemented on the homepage.
Displays product name, image, and price
Products are defined statically in HTML
Each product includes an “Add to Cart” button
JavaScript is used to handle user interactions and update the cart dynamically.
A search and filtering mechanism is implemented to improve usability.
Users can search products by name
Products can be filtered based on price range
JavaScript filters product data using conditions
DOM updates dynamically based on search input
The cart system is one of the core components of the application.
Cart data is stored in localStorage using a key (e.g., ep_cart)
User authentication is implemented using Supabase Auth.
Supabase APIs are used for authentication
Sessions are maintained to track logged-in users
Only authenticated users can proceed to checkout
Admin access is restricted based on database entries
The checkout process handles order confirmation and payment preparation.
Cart data is retrieved from localStorage
The checkout page prepares the system for payment processing.
The project uses a QR-based UPI payment system.
A UPI payment URL is generated using order details
QR code is generated using an external API
User scans the QR code using a payment app
User uploads a screenshot of the transaction
File is uploaded to Supabase Storage
Order data is stored in database
The database is managed using Supabase (PostgreSQL).
Supabase Storage is used for storing payment screenshots.
Each user has separate storage path
Security is implemented using multiple techniques:
Users can access only their own records
No sensitive data stored in frontend
An admin dashboard is developed for system control.
The application is deployed using GitHub Pages.
Testing was performed to ensure system reliability.
Errors were identified and corrected during testing to ensure smooth operation.
The development of this project involved the use of various technical resources and documentation to understand concepts and implement features effectively. The key references used are listed below:
Used for implementing authentication, database operations, storage, and Row Level Security (RLS).
Used for understanding HTML, CSS, and JavaScript concepts such as DOM manipulation, events, and localStorage.
Used for understanding the structure of UPI payment URLs and integrating QR-based payment functionality.
Used for generating dynamic QR codes for payment during checkout.
Used for understanding theoretical concepts related to e-commerce systems, database design, and security mechanisms.

The Elegance Perfumes E-Commerce Web Application successfully demonstrates the design and implementation of a complete online shopping system using modern web technologies. The project achieves its objective of creating an end-to-end platform where users can browse products, manage their cart, authenticate securely, and complete purchases through a structured checkout process.
One of the key highlights of this project is the use of a frontend-first architecture combined with Supabase as a Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS).

This approach simplifies backend development while still providing powerful features such as authentication, database management, and storage. It also makes the system lightweight, scalable, and easy to deploy.

The implementation of a QR-based UPI payment system adds practical value to the project by simulating real-world digital payment workflows. Although the payment verification is manual, it effectively demonstrates how transactions can be handled and validated in an e-commerce environment.

The inclusion of an admin panel further enhances the system by enabling order management and payment verification. The use of Row Level Security (RLS) ensures that data access is controlled and secure, which is a critical aspect of modern web applications.

Despite its successful implementation, the project has certain limitations such as the absence of automated payment gateways, inventory management, and email notification systems. However, these limitations also present opportunities for future enhancements and scalability.

In conclusion, this project provides a strong foundation in web development by integrating frontend design, backend services, authentication, payment processing, and security mechanisms into a single application. It not only fulfills academic requirements but also reflects real-world application design, making it a valuable learning experience and a solid base for future development.
